revision.text:
[[ja:秋祭り]] [[File:Aki Matsuri 2018.jpg|thumb|秋祭り]] '''Autumn Matsuri''' (秋祭り) refers to festivals held during the {{ill|Autumn|en|Autumn|qid=Q1314|lt=Autumn|1=Autumn}} season, collectively known as autumn festivals. These festivals are primarily celebrated in rural villages to give thanks for the {{ill|Harvest|en|Harvest|qid=Q213753|lt=Harvest|1=Harvest}}. In some cases, the [[Kinensai]] (祈年祭) held in February is referred to as a [[Spring Matsuri|Spring festival]], while the [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (新嘗祭) in November is considered an autumn festival. == Overview == [[File:播州の秋祭りの様子 (正八幡神社秋祭り).jpg|thumb|Banshū Autumn Matsuri
({{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}})]] Autumn is traditionally considered the "season of harvest," a time for gathering [[:en:grains|grains]] and [[:en:fruits|fruits]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://dictionary.goo.ne.jp/word/%E5%AE%9F%E3%82%8A%E3%81%AE%E7%A7%8B/ |title=実りの秋／稔りの秋（みのりのあき）の意味・使い方をわかりやすく解説 - goo国語辞書 |accessdate=2023-05-12|qq=}} Autumn festivals originated as events in which farmers expressed gratitude for bountiful harvests by "entertaining the gods of the fields" through music and dance in rural areas.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/465528/ |title=秋祭りとは？ 意味・内容、春夏秋冬の日本のまつりの特色 [暮らしの歳時記] All About |accessdate=2023-05-12|qq=}} Representative examples of autumn festivals include the [[Zuiki Matsuri at Kitano Tenmangu|Kitano Tenmangū Zuiki Matsuri]] in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, the [[Takachiho Yokagura Festival of Mythology|Takachiho Yokagura Festival]] in {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, the {{ill|Banshū Autumn Matsuri|ja|%E6%92%AD%E5%B7%9E%E3%81%AE%E7%A7%8B%E7%A5%AD%E3%82%8A|lt=Banshū Autumn Festival|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū Autumn Matsuri|qid=Q11498705|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri}} in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and the [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] held at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingū]] and other locations across Japan. == Notes == {{reflist|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|qid=Q4997332|lt=Bumper crop|1=Bumper crop}} * {{ill|Thanksgiving|en|Thanksgiving|qid=Q13959|lt=Thanksgiving|1=Thanksgiving}} * {{ill|Harvest festival|en|Harvest festival|qid=Q2304773|lt=Harvest festival|1=Harvest festival}} {{Event-stub|qq=}} * [[Spring Matsuri|Spring Festivals]] * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{translated page|ja|秋祭り|version=100703459|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:秋祭り]] [[ja:冬祭り]] [[ja:七夕]] [[ja:地域おこし]] [[ja:帰省]] [[ja:夏休み]] [[zh:%E5%A4%8F%E7%A5%AD]] [[ja:夏祭り]] {{Commons category|Autumn festivals in Japan}} {{Commons category|Summer festivals in Japan}} [[zh:夏祭]] {{wikidata link|Q11595783}} [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Autumn]] [[Category:秋のイベント]]
